config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society’s interests should automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse of power and silence minorities. In my view, individual rights are the foundation that protects people when the majority is wrong or unfair. Society matters, but it should be balanced with strong limits that prevent the collective from crushing the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu harus tetap menjadi prioritas utama, dan kepentingan masyarakat hanya boleh membatasinya dalam keadaan yang benar-benar perlu dan sangat sempit. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, diskriminasi, atau pembungkaman kelompok minoritas. Masyarakat memang penting, tetapi masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Kalau hak pribadi bisa dikalahkan begitu saja oleh klaim kepentingan umum, maka tidak ada jaminan nyata bagi kebebasan siapa pun.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because in practice that often becomes a license for officials or majorities to silence dissent. From an Indonesian perspective, I am very aware that appeals to “public interest” can be used to excuse unfair treatment, corruption, or pressure on minorities, so I am cautious about giving the collective too much power. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and social order do matter. The real principle should be that society can limit rights only in narrow, clearly justified ways, with strong checks so those limits are not abused.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu tetap harus didahulukan, dan kepentingan masyarakat hanya boleh membatasi hak itu dalam keadaan yang benar-benar sempit dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, saya tidak percaya pada gagasan bahwa “demi masyarakat” otomatis lebih sah, karena di sini alasan seperti itu terlalu sering dipakai untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara dan menekan kelompok yang tidak populer. Memang benar kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban sosial itu penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang membatasi kekuasaan kolektif agar tidak menggilas orang per orang. Kalau hak individu bisa dikorbankan terlalu mudah, maka yang dilindungi bukan masyarakat, melainkan mereka yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From Indonesia, I have seen how quickly “for the public good” can become a cover for arbitrary authority, corruption, or pressure on minorities, so I am very suspicious of giving the collective a blank check. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited, because public health, safety, and order sometimes require real restrictions. The key is that any limit on rights must be narrow, necessary, and tightly checked, otherwise society becomes an excuse to trample the very people it is supposed to serve.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar utama, dan kepentingan masyarakat hanya layak membatasi hak itu dalam situasi yang benar-benar sempit, perlu, dan bisa diuji. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, saya justru curiga ketika orang memakai alasan “demi masyarakat” karena itu sering menjadi jalan pintas untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara atau menekan orang yang tidak populer. Saya setuju bahwa kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban itu penting, tetapi itu tidak boleh dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengalahkan kebebasan pribadi. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik, yang paling berisiko bukan masyarakat, melainkan orang biasa yang haknya bisa diabaikan saat sedang tidak punya suara.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
